behaviors.description: Image with explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is whether the real vision model correctly identifies explicit content in the image. Mocking the model hides real classification errors, API response format issues, and the accuracy ceiling of the deployed model. Only a real vision model call can verify this behavior catches actual explicit images.
behaviors.test_description: Submit an image containing explicit content to the moderation pipeline, invoke the real vision model API, and verify the post is marked as flagged, queued for human review, and NOT routed to publication.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description calls real vision model (Agentic), matching the minimum level required.

behaviors.description: Text post with hate speech is flagged even when obfuscated (letter substitution, unicode lookalikes)
behaviors.justification: The failure is whether the real text model detects hate speech despite adversarial obfuscation techniques. Mocking a model that handles obfuscation does not answer the critical question: does the deployed model actually catch these patterns? This is testing the model's robustness to adversarial input, which only the real model can reveal.
behaviors.test_description: Submit text posts with obfuscated hate speech (e.g., 'h8e sp33ch', unicode lookalikes) to the moderation pipeline, invoke the real text model API, and verify the posts are flagged for review rather than approved.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test calls real text model (Agentic), matching the minimum level.

behaviors.description: Post passing both image and text checks is published, not held or silently dropped
behaviors.justification: Once model outputs (both 'safe') are available, the routing decision is deterministic. The failure is in the application logic: does the code correctly route to publish when both models pass? This is a logic bug, not a model accuracy issue. We can mock both models to return 'safe' and test the pipeline routing, catching publish-path failures without needing the real models.
behaviors.test_description: Mock both the vision and text models to return 'safe' classifications, submit a post through the moderation pipeline, and verify it appears in the published queue with correct metadata, not in hold or drop queues.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test mocks both models (Integration), matching the minimum level for testing routing logic.

behaviors.description: Full moderation pipeline (image check, text check, publish) completes within 5 seconds
behaviors.justification: Latency is measurable only with real API calls. Mocking both models would hide real network latency and AI model inference time, making the test artificially fast. The failure is 'real vision model + real text model + publish takes > 5 seconds'. This requires multiple agentic steps (vision→text→publish) executed sequentially in the real environment.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real image and text post to the production moderation pipeline with both real vision and text model APIs enabled, measure elapsed time from submission to publication, and assert total latency is less than 5 seconds. Run multiple times to capture realistic variance.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test calls both real models sequentially and measures end-to-end latency (Workflow level), matching the minimum needed to catch real-world timing failures.

behaviors.description: When vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the application's error-handling logic, not in the network layer or HTTP client resilience. Once we reliably simulate the vision model being unavailable (via exception mocking), the response path is deterministic. We can verify the queueing logic without needing a real unavailable API.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the vision model client to raise a connection timeout error, submit an image post to the pipeline, and verify the post is stored in a retry queue with retry metadata (attempt count, next retry time), not auto-approved to publication.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test mocks vision model error (Integration), matching the minimum level for error-handling logic.

behaviors.description: Moderation decision is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window
behaviors.justification: Real vision models may be non-deterministic on borderline images (floating-point precision, batch effects, GPU memory state). Mocking the model to return the same result twice hides the critical question: does the system cache decisions, or does it rely on lucky model consistency? We need the real model to test whether the system handles non-determinism via caching or fails when the model's second call returns a different confidence/classification.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image twice in quick succession (within the short window), invoking the real vision model both times, and verify both submissions receive identical moderation decisions (either both flagged or both published). If decision differs, verify it was due to documented model variance, not system bugs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test calls real vision model twice (Agentic), matching the minimum level to expose model non-determinism or caching logic failures.
